As per mail from CA:Apple does not disclose intermediate emission targets but a long-term goal. That is “to be carbon neutral across its entire value chain by 2030. Apple’s plan centres on an aggressive 75 percent reduction in overall carbon emissions from 2015 levels”. Based on the Environmental Progress Report, page 12, 2015 goss emissions were $38.4 million metric tons CO2e; so, by 2030 Apple plans to cut gross emissions to 9.6 million metric tons CO2e.Because ~98% of the gross emission are Scope 3 generated from product life cycle (see page 77 of the Environmental Report); we can infer that most plans are geared towards reducing Scope 3 emissions in the coming years.
